notes: DQ rules for the new 15-year payback-multiplier ROI columns introduced by docs/specs/roi-net-lifetime-value.md (lifetime_earnings_15yr, roi_raw_multiplier, roi_multiplier_basis). Closed-form constant 18.5989 = ((1.03^15 - 1) / 0.03). All rules apply to consumable.career_outcomes; the same columns are threaded through to consumable.program_career_paths via futureproof_engine.py and inherit these guarantees.

rules.name: lifetime_earnings_15yr NULL when earnings_1yr_median NULL
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_1yr_median IS NULL AND lifetime_earnings_15yr IS NOT NULL
rules.description: lifetime_earnings_15yr must be NULL whenever earnings_1yr_median is NULL. The closed-form formula has no other input; a non-null lifetime with null earnings indicates a transformer bug or stale row.

rules.name: lifetime_earnings_15yr NOT NULL when earnings_1yr_median NOT NULL
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL AND lifetime_earnings_15yr IS NULL
rules.description: lifetime_earnings_15yr must be NON-NULL whenever earnings_1yr_median is NON-NULL. The closed-form sum (earnings * 18.5989) is always computable.

rules.name: lifetime_earnings_15yr matches closed-form formula
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE earnings_1yr_median IS NOT NULL AND ABS(lifetime_earnings_15yr - earnings_1yr_median * 18.5989) >= 0.01
rules.description: Within rounding tolerance, lifetime_earnings_15yr must equal earnings_1yr_median * 18.5989. Drift indicates the multiplier constant has been changed without updating dependents.
rules.rationale: Spec roi-net-lifetime-value.md Decision #10 (flat 3% growth, 15-year window, closed-form constant).

rules.name: roi_raw_multiplier NULL when basis is 'none'
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE (lifetime_earnings_15yr IS NULL OR roi_multiplier_basis = 'none') AND roi_raw_multiplier IS NOT NULL
rules.description: roi_raw_multiplier must be NULL whenever lifetime_earnings_15yr is NULL or no usable cost basis is available (roi_multiplier_basis = 'none').

rules.name: roi_raw_multiplier NOT NULL when basis is sticker_4yr or net_price_4yr
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE roi_multiplier_basis IN ('sticker_4yr', 'net_price_4yr') AND roi_raw_multiplier IS NULL
rules.description: When provenance reports a usable cost basis, the multiplier must be present.

rules.name: roi_raw_multiplier within sanity range
rules.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE roi_raw_multiplier IS NOT NULL AND (roi_raw_multiplier <= 0 OR roi_raw_multiplier > 80)
rules.description: Non-null roi_raw_multiplier values must satisfy 0 < value <= 80. Values outside this range indicate cost or earnings outliers warranting review.
rules.rationale: Spec roi-net-lifetime-value.md §4 Data Model Changes (range guard).

rules.name: ROI distribution: median bucket in [4, 6]; extremes < 5%
rules.sql: WITH scored AS (SELECT CASE WHEN roi_raw_multiplier IS NULL THEN NULL WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 1.5 THEN 1 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 2.5 THEN 2 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 3.5 THEN 3 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 4.5 THEN 4 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 5.5 THEN 5 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 7.0 THEN 6 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 9.0 THEN 7 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 12.0 THEN 8 WHEN roi_raw_multiplier < 16.0 THEN 9 ELSE 10 END AS bucket FROM consumable.career_outcomes WHERE roi_raw_multiplier IS NOT NULL) SELECT bucket, COUNT(*) AS n, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS pct FROM scored GROUP BY bucket ORDER BY bucket
rules.threshold: median_bucket IN (4,5,6) AND pct(bucket=1) < 5 AND pct(bucket=10) < 5
rules.description: Calibration check on the threshold ladder. Median bucket (cumulative 50%) must fall in [4, 6]; bucket 1 and bucket 10 each < 5% of rows; no single bucket > 30%. Bounds-failure escalates to human before threshold tweaking.
